title: Add latent tyre-state features from a per-stint state-space model
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models lap time as a latent degradation state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than AR baselines.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models lap time as a latent degradation state with pit-stop resets and shows better predictive robustness than AR baselines. Your current features use raw degradation and ratios, but not an inferred hidden pace-loss state, its slope, or uncertainty.
implementation_hint: For each `Year,Race,Driver,Stint`, estimate a simple local-linear/Kalman state from `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `TyreLife`, and compound, with reset after `PitStop=1`. Feed the filtered state, one-step forecast residual, state slope, and posterior variance into trees.
expected_effect: Can sharpen the pit-window signal when raw `Cumulative_Degradation` is noisy or non-monotone, especially on long stints and wet compounds.
risk: A poorly tuned filter can just restate existing features and add noise; leakage-free fitting must be strictly past-only within sequence.
